    $29 at Amazon

Best storage options for the Steam Deck: Bottom line

These are the best storage options for the Steam Deck in 2024. We covered a traditional SSD like the SABRENT Rocket 2230 NVMe 4.0 1TB SSD which offers the fastest possible read and write speeds for your games. We also looked at several popular microSD cards, like the SanDisk Extreme, which offers 1TB of storage, the Samsung Evo Select, which is a value microSD card, and even the SanDisk Extreme Pro, which is the fastest possible SD card you can buy.

The most important thing when picking out a microSD card on Amazon is to make sure that you get one from a verified seller. Online there are plenty of fake microSD cards being sold that report higher storage but will actually fill up and not accept any more data, or start overwriting existing data, way earlier than you expect. This is why we suggest you pick from a trusted vendor.

If you don't already own a Steam Deck, you can check out our Steam Deck review for more information. It might not be one of the best gaming laptops, but it's still an excellent way to game on the go.

Steam Deck

Valve's first portable gaming PC, the Steam Deck is one of the best gaming devices on the market, giving you access to a huge library of games you can play anywhere you go.

$399 at Steam
item.content:encoded:
Do you have a Steam Deck and need a way to pack it full of all your games? There are two ways to expand the storage capacity of this portable console and change the way you play games. One is through the use of microSD cards and the other is via replacement of the internal SSD. The easiest method is adding a microSD card though, which is the same form of storage that can be used to expand the capacity of many tablets and smartphones. The microSD card can be any size, but it should support the UHS-I speed standard, which most microSD cards support these days.

Related
How to replace the SSD in the Steam Deck

Although the Steam Deck's higher-end models come with SSDs, they're pretty expensive. Here's how to install your own for less.

If the goal is to provide as much storage room for games as possible, we'd recommend going down the route of replacing the internal SSD. It's worth noting that this will void your warranty, but it is perfectly safe, so long as you follow the necessary steps and cause no damage to the internal components of the Steam Deck. You'll need to buy an M.2 2230 NVMe SSD, which we're suggesting below. Remember, have operating system installation files handy, a backup of your existing files, and be prepared to reinstall a fresh OS after swapping the SSD.

  • Editor's choice
    Western Digital WD Black SN770M
    $110 $130 Save $20

    The WD Black SN770M is one of the fastest SSDs in this form factor that you can get, and even if it's limited to PCIe 3.0 speeds in the Steam Deck it will handle loading your games at the maximum the console allows.

    $110 at Best Buy $110 at Amazon (1TB) $206 at Amazon (2TB)
  • Fastest speeds
    Crucial P310
    $215 $265 Save $50

    The newest 2230 form-factor SSD is also the fastest, boasting speeds of up to 7,100MB/s thanks to the latest 232-layer Micron QLC NAND. That'll make your Steam Deck games load slightly quicker, although it'll only be running at PCIe 3.0 speeds.

    $115 at Amazon (1TB) $215 at Amazon (2TB) $215 at Crucial
  • Best value
    XPC Technologies 2TB M.2 2230 NVMe PCIe SSD

    XPC Technologies's 2230-sized SSD is a rebranded SN740 from Western Digital that can fit into handheld gaming PCs like the Steam Deck and ROG Ally. It's rated for reads of up to 5,100MB/s and writes of 4,800MB/s and comes with up to 2TB of storage capacity.

    $78 at Amazon (1TB) $145 at Amazon (2TB)
  • Premium pick
    SABRENT Rocket 2230 NVMe 4.0 1TB SSD

    If you want to expand your Steam Deck storage and don't want to deal with slow read/write speeds, check out the Sabrent Rocket 2230 NVMe SSD. It offers up to 4,750MB/s read and 4,300 MB/s write speeds and unmatched reliability that you won't get with a microSD card.

    $100 at B&H $100 at Amazon
  • Mid-tier performance
    Corsair MP600 Mini

    The Corsair MP600 Mini is a great SSD that you can put in the Steam Deck, and it packs up to 2TB of storage that you can fill up with as many games as you'd like. It's also got speeds up to 4,800MB/s for reads and writes, so it's somewhere in the middle of the pack.

    $86 at Amazon (1TB) $230 at Amazon (2TB)
  • Affordable capacity
    Silicon Power Gen4 M.2 SSD
    $73 $90 Save $17

    The Silicon Power 2230 SSD is one of the more affordable SSDs for the Steam Deck. It is under $100, but it still has read speeds of up to 4,900MB/s and write speeds of up to 3,200MB/s. It also has a 5-year limited warranty.

    $73 at Amazon (1TB) $138 at Amazon (2TB)
  • Plenty of space
    SanDisk Extreme microSD card

    Storing your Steam library takes up a lot of space, so add up to 1TB of storage to the Steam Deck with this microSD card from SanDisk. That way, all you have to think about is what to play next.

    $93 at Amazon
  • Affordable durable microSD card
    Kingston Canvas Go Plus microSD card

    The Kingston Canvas Go Plus is an affordable microSD card with data transfer speeds of up to 170 MB/s. The card supports UHS Speed Class 3 speeds and has the A2 app performance rating. It's also waterproof, and vibration and shockproof, too.

    $16 at Amazon
  • Speedy microSD storage
    SanDisk Extreme Pro microSD card

    The SanDisk Extreme Pro microSD card is what the name suggests. It's a microSD card that can survive the most extreme conditions. It's temperate-proof, waterproof, and X-ray-proof. Not to forget it supports the SanDisk QuickFlow technology for 200MB/s read speeds and 140MB/s write speeds

    $29 at Amazon

Best storage options for the Steam Deck: Bottom line

These are the best storage options for the Steam Deck in 2024. We covered a traditional SSD like the SABRENT Rocket 2230 NVMe 4.0 1TB SSD which offers the fastest possible read and write speeds for your games. We also looked at several popular microSD cards, like the SanDisk Extreme, which offers 1TB of storage, the Samsung Evo Select, which is a value microSD card, and even the SanDisk Extreme Pro, which is the fastest possible SD card you can buy.

The most important thing when picking out a microSD card on Amazon is to make sure that you get one from a verified seller. Online there are plenty of fake microSD cards being sold that report higher storage but will actually fill up and not accept any more data, or start overwriting existing data, way earlier than you expect. This is why we suggest you pick from a trusted vendor.

If you don't already own a Steam Deck, you can check out our Steam Deck review for more information. It might not be one of the best gaming laptops, but it's still an excellent way to game on the go.

Steam Deck

Valve's first portable gaming PC, the Steam Deck is one of the best gaming devices on the market, giving you access to a huge library of games you can play anywhere you go.

$399 at Steam

item.title: How to change the date format on Windows 11
item.itunes:title: How to change the date format on Windows 11
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Windows 11 shows you the time and date at all times on the bottom right corner of your screen, and usually, it's formatted to match the region you choose during the setup process. For most people, that makes sense, since that will be the format you're most familiar with. But what if you don't like it?

If your PC's region is set to the U.S., you'll see the date in the MM/DD/YYYY format, but as anybody in Europe will tell you, DD/MM/YYYY is the best format. So here's what you need to get the right date and time format on your Windows PC.

Related
How to enable the seconds on the Windows 11 clock

Want to keep track of time more accurately? The Windows 11 Moment 3 update makes it easy.

How to change the date format to another country

The easiest method

If you want to use a date and time format used in a specific country, you can change it relatively easily. Here's how:

  1. Open the Settings app. You can find it in the Start menu or by using the Windows search bar.
  2. Go to Time & language on the left-side menu.
  3. Click Language and region.
  4. Click the dropdown menu next to Regional format (it should say Recommended).
  5. Choose the regional format for the country you want.

This method only lets you choose regional formats for the languages you have installed on your PC. For example, if your PC is in English and that's the only language you ahve installed, you can choose the regional format used in the United States or the one used in the U.K. If you want to use a format from a country that speaks a different language, you first need to add that language to Windows with the Add a language button near the top of the page.

1:52
Related
How to change or tweak keyboard layouts in Windows 11

The Windows 11 settings app makes it easy to change your keyboard layout in just a few clicks.

Use custom date and time settings

Find your specific preference

Another option you have is to fully customize the time and date settings, which can also be done in the Settings app.

  1. Open the Settings app.
  2. Go to Time & language on the left-side menu.
  3. Click Language and region.
  4. Expand the Regional format field by clicking it (don't click the dropdown menu inside it).
  5. Click Change formats.
  6. Use the First day of the week dropdown menu to choose which day of the week should be first on a calendar.
  7. Click the Short date dropdown menu to change the format for short dates (such as in the taskbar).
  8. Click the Long date dropdown menu to change the long date format.
  9. If you'd also like to change the time format, do the same for Short time and Long time.

This makes it possible to get the ideal time and date format for you without having to add more languages to your PC, even if it takes a bit more work.

Related
How to use the Settings app on Windows 11

The new Settings app on WIndows 11 houses a ton of options. Here's everything you can find inside it and what it does.

How to change the date format using Control Panel

For old-school Windows users

  1. Open the Start menu and search for Control Panel. Choose the first option.
  2. Switch the View by option (in the top right) to either Large icons or Small icons.
  3. Click Region.
  4. Use the Short date, Long date, Short time, Long time, and First day of the week dropdown menus to choose the right format for each one.
  5. Preview the changes under Examples.
  6. Click OK to confirm your changes.

Get the right time and date for you

At the end of the day, either of these methods will allow you to see the time and date on your laptop in whatever format you prefer. The Settings app is definitely the most accessible option, but if you're more familiar with the Control Panel and prefer it, that's still around, too.
